label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out if Sal tells the truth based on the statements given. Let me break down each person's statement and see how they relate to each other. First, Willian tells the truth. That's a given. So Willian is a truth-teller. Then Michaela says that Willian lies. Wait, but if Willian tells the truth, then Michaela's statement is false. So Michaela must be lying. Next, Conception says Michaela lies. Well, since we just determined that Michaela does lie (because she said Willian lies, which is false), Conception is actually telling the truth. So Conception's statement is true. Then, Inga says that Conception tells the truth. Since Conception is indeed telling the truth, Inga's statement is also true. Therefore, Inga is a truth-teller. Finally, Sal says Inga lies. But if Inga is telling the truth, then Sal's statement is false. That would mean Sal is lying. Therefore, Sal does not tell the truth; he lies. Wait, let me check again to make sure I didn't make any mistakes. Starting from the top: 1. Willian tells the truth. (Given) 2. Michaela says Willian lies. Since Willian is truthful, Michaela's statement is false. So Michaela lies. 3. Conception says Michaela lies. That's true, so Conception tells the truth. 4. Inga says Conception tells the truth. Since Conception does tell the truth, Inga is truthful. 5. Sal says Inga lies. But Inga is truthful, so Sal is lying. Therefore, Sal does not tell the truth. Yes,
